title: Toy Story (1995)
plot_summary: Toy Story follows the adventures of a group of toys that come to life when their owner, Andy, is not around. The leader of the toys, Woody, gets jealous when a new spaceman toy, Buzz Lightyear, becomes Andy's favorite. The two toys end up lost and must work together to find their way back home, all while navigating through various obstacles and challenges. Through their journey, Woody and Buzz learn the true meaning of friendship and loyalty.
stars: Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Don Rickles

title: Jumanji (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Jumanji', a magical board game unleashes a world of adventure and chaos when two siblings discover it in an old mansion. Every roll of the dice leads to unpredictable and dangerous events that must be faced in order to finish the game and set things right. As the game's jungle-themed challenges come to life, the players must work together to survive and outsmart the game's traps. With time running out and the stakes high, they must find a way to win before they are trapped in the game forever.
stars: Robin Williams, Kirsten Dunst, Bonnie Hunt

title: Grumpier Old Men (1995)
plot_summary: In this sequel to 'Grumpy Old Men', lifelong frenemies John Gustafson and Max Goldman continue their comedic rivalry in a small Minnesota town. When the vivacious Maria Ragetti moves in next door, both men try to win her affection, leading to hilarious antics and schemes. Meanwhile, John's daughter Melanie becomes engaged, causing further tension between the two old friends. As they navigate love, family, and aging, John and Max ultimately come to realize the true value of their friendship.
stars: Jack Lemmon, Walter Matthau, Sophia Loren

title: Waiting to Exhale (1995)
plot_summary: Waiting to Exhale is a drama film that follows the lives of four African-American women living in Phoenix, Arizona. Each woman is dealing with various personal and romantic struggles, including infidelity, single motherhood, and finding self-worth. As they navigate their challenges, the four friends rely on each other for support and understanding, forming a strong bond that helps them through difficult times. The film explores themes of friendship, love, and empowerment as the women confront their pasts and strive to create better futures.
stars: Whitney Houston, Angela Bassett, Loretta Devine

title: Father of the Bride Part II (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Father of the Bride Part II', George Banks is shocked to learn that his wife Nina is pregnant, just as their daughter Annie is also expecting a baby. George struggles to come to terms with becoming a father again at his age, while dealing with the chaos of planning two simultaneous baby showers and managing the expectations of his family. As the due dates approach, George must navigate the challenges of impending fatherhood once more, balancing his own anxieties with the excitement and love that comes with expanding his family.
stars: Steve Martin, Diane Keaton, Martin Short

title: Heat (1995)
plot_summary: Heat (1995) is a crime thriller directed by Michael Mann. The film follows the cat-and-mouse game between a brilliant master thief, Neil McCauley (portrayed by Robert De Niro), and an obsessive LAPD detective, Vincent Hanna (played by Al Pacino). As McCauley plans a major heist, Hanna is determined to bring him to justice. The intense and gripping narrative explores the complex dynamics between the two characters as their paths inevitably converge, leading to a dramatic and memorable showdown.
stars: Robert De Niro, Al Pacino, Val Kilmer

title: Sabrina (1995)
plot_summary: Sabrina is a romantic comedy about a chauffeur's daughter named Sabrina Fairchild who is in love with her employer's playboy son, David. After a trip to Paris transforms Sabrina into an elegant and confident woman, she catches the attention of David, who is engaged to another woman. As Sabrina and David's relationship blossoms, complications arise, including Sabrina's longstanding crush on David's responsible older brother, Linus. Directed by Sydney Pollack, the film explores themes of love, self-discovery, and the complexities of relationships.
stars: Harrison Ford, Julia Ormond, Greg Kinnear

title: Tom and Huck (1995)
plot_summary: Tom and Huck is a 1995 American adventure comedy-drama film based on Mark Twain's novel The Adventures of Tom Sawyer. The story follows Tom Sawyer and Huckleberry Finn as they witness a murder and must go on the run to escape the killer. Along the way, they have various adventures and develop a strong friendship. The film explores themes of friendship, loyalty, and the innocence of childhood, set against a backdrop of small-town America in the 19th century.
stars: Jonathan Taylor Thomas, Brad Renfro, Eric Schweig

title: Sudden Death (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Sudden Death', a former firefighter turned fire inspector finds himself in a deadly game of cat-and-mouse with terrorists who have taken over a hockey arena during the Stanley Cup Finals. Racing against time, he must not only save the hostages, including his own children, but also prevent a catastrophic explosion that could kill thousands. With tension mounting and stakes high, he must use his skills and resourcefulness to outsmart the terrorists and save the day.
stars: Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J. Barry

title: GoldenEye (1995)
plot_summary: In 'GoldenEye,' James Bond must stop a former MI6 agent, Alec Trevelyan, from using a powerful satellite weapon to destroy London. Along the way, Bond teams up with Russian computer programmer Natalya Simonova to uncover the conspiracy and take down the villain. As Bond races against time, he faces intense action sequences, thrilling chase scenes, and a climactic showdown atop the satellite dish. The film combines high-stakes espionage with classic Bond charm and wit, making it a standout entry in the franchise.
stars: Pierce Brosnan, Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco

title: American President, The (1995)
plot_summary: The American President follows widowed U.S. President Andrew Shepherd as he navigates the challenges of leading the country while also falling in love with an environmental lobbyist named Sydney Ellen Wade. As their relationship blossoms, President Shepherd faces political backlash and pressure from his staff, ultimately having to decide between pursuing love or his political career.
stars: Michael Douglas, Annette Bening, Martin Sheen

title: Dracula: Dead and Loving It (1995)
plot_summary: In this horror-comedy spoof, Count Dracula is brought back to life in 19th-century England and wreaks havoc in his attempt to turn the locals into his undead minions. As chaos ensues, a group of vampire hunters led by Dr. Van Helsing try to stop him. The movie is filled with slapstick humor and parodies classic vampire lore, making fun of various horror tropes along the way.
stars: Leslie Nielsen, Mel Brooks, Peter MacNicol

title: Balto (1995)
plot_summary: Balto is a animated adventure film based on a true story about a half-wolf, half-dog who embarks on a treacherous journey to deliver medicine to a remote Alaskan town suffering from a deadly epidemic. Along the way, Balto faces various challenges and learns the true meaning of heroism and determination. The film showcases themes of courage, friendship, and perseverance, making it a heartwarming and inspiring story for viewers of all ages.
stars: Kevin Bacon, Bob Hoskins, Bridget Fonda

title: Nixon (1995)
plot_summary: Nixon is a biographical drama that follows the political career of Richard Nixon, from his early years to his presidency and eventual resignation in the wake of the Watergate scandal. The film delves into Nixon's complex personality, his struggles with power and ambition, and the turbulent times of the 1960s and 70s. It portrays the highs and lows of his presidency, including his foreign policy achievements and domestic controversies.
stars: Anthony Hopkins, Joan Allen, Powers Boothe

title: Cutthroat Island (1995)
plot_summary: In this action-adventure film, Morgan Adams, a fearless female pirate, sets out on a high-seas treasure hunt to find the three parts of a map that will lead her to the legendary Cutthroat Island. Along the way, she teams up with her ex-lover and a slave who knows the whereabouts of the final piece. However, they face numerous obstacles including rival pirates and treacherous waters in their quest for the hidden treasure.
stars: Geena Davis, Matthew Modine, Frank Langella

title: Casino (1995)
plot_summary: Casino is a crime film directed by Martin Scorsese that follows the rise and fall of mobster Sam 'Ace' Rothstein in the world of gambling and mafia-controlled casinos in Las Vegas. As Rothstein navigates the treacherous underworld, he faces challenges from his volatile friend Nicky Santoro and his tumultuous relationship with his wife Ginger. The movie delves into themes of power, greed, loyalty, and betrayal.
stars: Robert De Niro, Sharon Stone, Joe Pesci

title: Sense and Sensibility (1995)
plot_summary: Sense and Sensibility, directed by Ang Lee, is a period drama based on Jane Austen's novel. The story revolves around the Dashwood sisters, Elinor and Marianne, as they navigate love, heartbreak, and societal expectations in 19th century England. The film explores themes of class, romance, and the resilience of the human spirit.
stars: Emma Thompson, Kate Winslet, Hugh Grant

title: Four Rooms (1995)
plot_summary: Four Rooms is a comedy anthology film directed by Quentin Tarantino and three other directors. The movie consists of four segments, each depicting bizarre and outrageous events that take place in a hotel on New Year's Eve. The quirky characters and absurd situations create a darkly humorous and chaotic narrative.
stars: Tim Roth, Amanda De Cadenet, David Proval

title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995)
plot_summary: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls is a comedy film directed by Steve Oedekerk. In this sequel, eccentric pet detective Ace Ventura embarks on a wild adventure to Africa to recover a sacred bat and rescue a tribe's missing warrior. The movie is filled with slapstick humor, zany antics, and Jim Carrey's signature comedic performance.
stars: Jim Carrey, Ian McNeice, Simon Callow

title: Money Train (1995)
plot_summary: Money Train is an action thriller directed by Joseph Ruben that follows two foster brothers, John and Charlie, who work as transit cops and plan to rob the 'money train' that collects subway revenue. As their heist unfolds, they face obstacles from their supervisor, a love interest, and their own conflicting motives. The film blends suspense, humor, and camaraderie.
stars: Wesley Snipes, Woody Harrelson, Jennifer Lopez

title: Get Shorty (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Get Shorty', a Miami loan shark travels to Hollywood to collect a debt and ends up getting involved in the movie business. As he navigates the cutthroat world of filmmaking, he discovers his talent for producing exceeds his debt collection skills. With a mix of humor and crime, the protagonist finds himself in a complex web of relationships and power struggles.
stars: John Travolta, Gene Hackman, Rene Russo

title: Copycat (1995)
plot_summary: 'Copycat' follows a criminal psychologist and a detective as they hunt down a serial killer who mimics famous murderers from the past. The tension rises as the killer targets the psychologist, forcing her to confront her own fears and past traumas in a battle of wits and survival.
stars: Sigourney Weaver, Holly Hunter, Dermot Mulroney

title: Assassins (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Assassins', two rival hitmen, one seasoned and one young and ambitious, find themselves on a collision course as they pursue the same target. Betrayal, intrigue, and action-packed sequences ensue as the assassins engage in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
stars: Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas, Julianne Moore

title: Powder (1995)
plot_summary: 'Powder' tells the story of a young albino man with unique electromagnetic abilities who faces prejudice and misunderstanding in a small town. As he tries to find his place in the world, he forms deep connections with those who see beyond his appearance and embrace his extraordinary gifts.
stars: Mary Steenburgen, Sean Patrick Flanery, Jeff Goldblum

title: Leaving Las Vegas (1995)
plot_summary: In 'Leaving Las Vegas', a self-destructive alcoholic screenwriter heads to Las Vegas with the intent to drink himself to death. There, he meets a prostitute who becomes his unlikely companion in a journey of despair, love, and redemption. Their tumultuous relationship explores themes of addiction, loneliness, and human connection.
stars: Nicolas Cage, Elisabeth Shue, Julian Sands

title: Othello (1995)
plot_summary: Othello is a 1995 film adaptation of William Shakespeare's play of the same name. Directed by Oliver Parker, the movie follows the tragic story of Othello, a Moorish general in the Venetian army, and his ensign Iago who manipulates him into believing his wife Desdemona is unfaithful. Betrayal, jealousy, and revenge drive the characters to their fateful ends in this classic tale of love and deceit.
stars: Lawrence Fishburne, Kenneth Branagh, Irene Jacob

title: Now and Then (1995)
plot_summary: Now and Then is a 1995 coming-of-age film directed by Lesli Linka Glatter. The story revolves around four childhood friends who reunite as adults and reminisce about a life-changing summer in 1970. The movie explores themes of friendship, love, loss, and the passage of time as the women confront their past and present selves.
stars: Christina Ricci, Rosie O'Donnell, Thora Birch

title: Persuasion (1995)
plot_summary: Persuasion is a 1995 adaptation of Jane Austen's novel of the same name. Directed by Roger Michell, the film follows Anne Elliot, a young woman who is persuaded to break off her engagement to a naval officer due to societal pressures. Years later, they meet again, and Anne must navigate her feelings and societal expectations. The movie explores themes of second chances, love, and societal norms.
stars: Amanda Root, Ciarán Hinds, Susan Fleetwood

title: City of Lost Children, The (Cité des enfants perdus, La) (1995)
plot_summary: The City of Lost Children is a 1995 dark fantasy film directed by Marc Caro and Jean-Pierre Jeunet. Set in a surreal and dystopian world, the story follows a mad scientist who kidnaps children to steal their dreams. A circus strongman, One, sets out to rescue his adopted brother from the scientist's clutches. The film is a visually stunning and imaginative tale of innocence, loss, and wonder.
stars: Ron Perlman, Daniel Emilfork, Judith Vittet

title: Shanghai Triad (Yao a yao yao dao waipo qiao) (1995)
plot_summary: Shanghai Triad is a 1995 crime drama directed by Zhang Yimou. Set in 1930s Shanghai, the film follows a young boy who becomes the servant of a powerful triad boss. As he navigates the dangerous and opulent world of the triad, he uncovers dark secrets and betrayals that threaten his life and loyalty. The movie explores themes of power, loyalty, and the consequences of choices.
stars: Gong Li, Li Baotian, Wang Xiaoxiao

title: Dangerous Minds (1995)
plot_summary: Dangerous Minds is a drama film about a former Marine who takes on a teaching job at an inner-city high school. She struggles to connect with her students, who are from tough backgrounds and have little interest in learning. Through her unconventional teaching methods, she inspires her students to believe in themselves and strive for a better future. The film explores themes of education, race, and empowerment.
stars: Michelle Pfeiffer, George Dzundza, Courtney B. Vance

title: Twelve Monkeys (a.k.a. 12 Monkeys) (1995)
plot_summary: Twelve Monkeys is a science fiction film that follows the story of a convict from a post-apocalyptic future who is sent back in time to gather information about a deadly virus that wiped out most of humanity. As he navigates the past, he encounters a psychiatrist and a group of activists. The film weaves a complex narrative of time travel, madness, and fate.
stars: Bruce Willis, Madeleine Stowe, Brad Pitt

title: Wings of Courage (1995)
plot_summary: Wings of Courage is a biographical drama that tells the story of the pioneers of aviation, Antoine de Saint-Exupéry and Jean Mermoz. The film explores their daring adventures as they push the boundaries of flight in South America. It delves into themes of courage, passion, and the pursuit of dreams.
stars: Craig Sheffer, Val Kilmer, Elizabeth McGovern

title: Babe (1995)
plot_summary: Babe is a heartwarming family film about a pig who dreams of becoming a sheepdog. Raised by a border collie on a farm, Babe defies expectations and wins over the other animals with his kindness and determination. The film celebrates the power of friendship, acceptance, and believing in oneself.
stars: James Cromwell, Magda Szubanski, Christine Cavanaugh

title: Carrington (1995)
plot_summary: Carrington is a biographical drama that explores the unconventional relationship between painter Dora Carrington and writer Lytton Strachey in early 20th-century England. The film delves into their complex bond, artistic pursuits, and the challenges they face in a society bound by conventions. It offers a poignant portrayal of love, art, and individuality.
stars: Emma Thompson, Jonathan Pryce, Steven Waddington

title: Dead Man Walking (1995)
plot_summary: Dead Man Walking is a powerful drama based on a true story. It follows the journey of Sister Helen Prejean, a nun who becomes a spiritual advisor to a death row inmate, Matthew Poncelet. As Sister Helen tries to help Matthew come to terms with his crimes and imminent execution, she also grapples with her own beliefs about forgiveness and justice. The film explores themes of redemption, compassion, and the complexities of the justice system.
stars: Susan Sarandon, Sean Penn, Robert Prosky

title: Across the Sea of Time (1995)
plot_summary: Across the Sea of Time is a visually stunning adventure that follows the story of a young Russian immigrant boy, Tomas, who travels to America in search of his long-lost family. Through his journey, Tomas discovers the wonders of New York City and the history of immigration. The film combines elements of drama and documentary to showcase the beauty and challenges of cultural diversity and the immigrant experience.
stars: Peter Reznik, Caroline Goodall, Michael Chapman

title: It Takes Two (1995)
plot_summary: It Takes Two is a heartwarming family comedy about two identical girls, Amanda and Alyssa, who switch lives in order to bring their single parents together. The girls team up to outsmart their adults and create opportunities for love and happiness. Along the way, they learn valuable lessons about family, friendship, and the importance of communication. The film is a delightful mix of humor and heartfelt moments.
stars: Kirstie Alley, Steve Guttenberg, Mary-Kate Olsen

title: Clueless (1995)
plot_summary: Clueless is a modern retelling of Jane Austen's novel Emma, set in a Beverly Hills high school. The film follows the fashionable and popular Cher Horowitz as she navigates the ups and downs of teenage life, friendship, and romance. With her friends by her side, Cher learns valuable lessons about empathy, self-discovery, and the true meaning of love. Clueless is a beloved teen comedy known for its witty dialogue and iconic fashion.
stars: Alicia Silverstone, Stacey Dash, Brittany Murphy

title: Cry, the Beloved Country (1995)
plot_summary: Cry, the Beloved Country is a poignant drama set in South Africa during the apartheid era. The film tells the story of a Zulu pastor, Stephen Kumalo, who travels to Johannesburg to search for his missing son, Absalom. As Stephen delves into the harsh realities of urban life and racial injustice, he grapples with his own beliefs and the legacy of his family. The film explores themes of forgiveness, reconciliation, and the enduring power of hope.
stars: James Earl Jones, Richard Harris, Vusi Kunene

title: Richard III (1995)
plot_summary: In this adaptation of William Shakespeare's play, Richard III, Ian McKellen portrays the ruthless and power-hungry King Richard III in a modern-day setting. The film follows Richard's manipulative and deceitful schemes to ascend the throne of England, leading to betrayal and violence in his path to power. As Richard's reign becomes more tyrannical, his enemies close in, ultimately resulting in a climactic battle for control of the kingdom.
stars: Ian McKellen, Annette Bening, Jim Broadbent

title: Dead Presidents (1995)
plot_summary: Directed by Albert Hughes and Allen Hughes, Dead Presidents follows the story of Anthony Curtis, a young African American man who struggles to reintegrate into society after returning from the Vietnam War. Facing economic hardship and discrimination, Anthony joins a group of friends to execute a daring heist to secure their future. The film delves into themes of race, poverty, and the impact of war on individuals and communities.
stars: Larenz Tate, Keith David, Chris Tucker

title: Restoration (1995)
plot_summary: Set in the 17th century, Restoration tells the story of Robert Merivel, a talented young physician who is summoned to the court of King Charles II. Merivel's life takes a dramatic turn as he becomes the King's personal physician and is granted wealth and privilege. However, his newfound status comes at a cost as he grapples with love, betrayal, and the fragility of power in a turbulent era of political intrigue and social change.
stars: Robert Downey Jr., Sam Neill, David Thewlis

title: Mortal Kombat (1995)
plot_summary: Based on the popular video game franchise, Mortal Kombat follows a group of Earth's greatest warriors who are summoned to a mystical island to compete in a tournament that will determine the fate of the world. As they battle against powerful foes and supernatural forces, the fighters must discover their inner strength and overcome their personal demons to emerge victorious. The fate of humanity hangs in the balance as they face the ultimate challenge.
stars: Christopher Lambert, Robin Shou, Linden Ashby

title: To Die For (1995)
plot_summary: Directed by Gus Van Sant, To Die For is a dark comedy that follows the ambitious and manipulative Suzanne Stone as she pursues her dream of becoming a famous news anchor. Suzanne's ruthless ambition leads her to orchestrate a sinister plot that involves her husband's murder, setting off a chain of events that spiral out of control. The film explores themes of media obsession, fame, and the destructive nature of unchecked ambition.
stars: Nicole Kidman, Matt Dillon, Joaquin Phoenix

title: How to Make an American Quilt (1995)
plot_summary: How to Make an American Quilt is a drama film directed by Jocelyn Moorhouse. The movie follows the story of a young woman who spends a summer with her grandmother and her quilting bee, learning about love and life through the tales of the women around her. As she contemplates her own future, she finds solace and wisdom in the stories shared by the women in the quilting circle.
stars: Winona Ryder, Ellen Burstyn, Anne Bancroft

title: Seven (a.k.a. Se7en) (1995)
plot_summary: Seven, also known as Se7en, is a psychological thriller directed by David Fincher. The film follows two detectives, a rookie and a veteran, as they hunt down a serial killer who uses the seven deadly sins as his modus operandi. As the detectives get closer to the killer, they are drawn into a dark and twisted game that challenges their beliefs and morals.
stars: Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, Kevin Spacey

title: Pocahontas (1995)
plot_summary: Pocahontas is an animated musical film directed by Mike Gabriel and Eric Goldberg. The movie tells the fictionalized story of the encounter between Native American woman Pocahontas and Englishman John Smith. Against a backdrop of cultural differences and prejudices, Pocahontas and John Smith find themselves drawn to each other, challenging the norms of their societies.
stars: Irene Bedard, Mel Gibson, David Ogden Stiers

title: When Night Is Falling (1995)
plot_summary: When Night Is Falling is a romantic drama film directed by Patricia Rozema. The movie follows the story of a university professor whose life is turned upside down when she falls in love with a circus performer. As she navigates the complexities of her newfound relationship and her own desires, she is forced to confront her own beliefs and fears.
stars: Pascale Bussières, Rachael Crawford, Henry Czerny

title: Usual Suspects, The (1995)
plot_summary: The Usual Suspects is a neo-noir mystery film directed by Bryan Singer. The movie follows the interrogation of a conman named Verbal Kint, who recounts the events leading up to a deadly heist orchestrated by the mysterious criminal mastermind Keyser Söze. As the pieces of the puzzle come together, the truth becomes increasingly elusive.
stars: Kevin Spacey, Gabriel Byrne, Chazz Palminteri
